assertions.title: Each major flow doc identifies canonical vs non-canonical paths

steps.action: Read FLOW-INVENTORY.md to identify all 11 major flow packets
steps.observed: 11 flow packets identified: auth-identity, tenant-admin-management, catalog-setup-readiness, manufacturing-packing, inventory-management, order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, invoice-dealer-finance, accounting-period-close, hr-payroll, reporting-export

steps.action: Grep all flow packets for 'Canonical vs Non-Canonical' section headers
steps.expected: Each flow packet has a dedicated canonical vs non-canonical section
steps.observed: All 11 flow packets contain '## 6. Canonical vs Non-Canonical Paths' with both '### Canonical Paths' and '### Non-Canonical / Deprecated Paths' subsections

steps.action: Verify canonical paths identify owning module/controller and entrypoint ownership
steps.expected: Each canonical path names the owning controller/module
steps.observed: Every canonical path table includes Owner column (e.g., AuthController/AuthService, SalesController, DispatchController, PurchasingWorkflowController, HrPayrollController, etc.)

steps.action: Verify non-canonical paths include replacement notes or explicit status
steps.expected: Each deprecated path has a replacement or 'no replacement' note
steps.observed: All non-canonical paths include Status (Deprecated, Retired, Non-canonical) and Replacement column pointing to canonical alternatives or noting no replacement exists

steps.action: Verify cross-module boundary notes explain which module owns entrypoint vs downstream side effect
steps.expected: Cross-Module Dependencies section in each packet identifies module ownership and direction
steps.observed: All 11 flow packets have '## 7. Cross-Module Dependencies' with Module, Dependency, and Direction columns explaining entrypoint ownership and downstream side-effect ownership

canonicalNonCanonicalSections.auth-identity.md: Section 6: 4 canonical paths (login, refresh, logout, password reset, MFA) owned by AuthController/AuthService/PasswordResetService/MfaService; 3 non-canonical (legacy super-admin alias, email-only recovery, companyId alias)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.tenant-admin-management.md: Section 6: 7 canonical paths (onboarding, lifecycle, limits, modules, user CRUD); 1 non-canonical (shared /api/v1/support/** → use host-specific)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.catalog-setup-readiness.md: Section 6: 4 canonical paths (item creation, import, packaging mappings, readiness); 3 retired (product endpoints hard-cut to items)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.manufacturing-packing.md: Section 6: 4 canonical paths (production logs, packing records, cost allocation, packaging mappings); 4 non-canonical (production-batches retired, /factory/pack retired, PackingCompletionService partial)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.inventory-management.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (FG adjustments, RM adjustments, opening stock, batch traceability, dispatch); 2 non-canonical (legacy dispatch, legacy intake via toggle)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.order-to-cash.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (order creation, confirmation, dispatch, credit requests, dealer portal credit); 4 non-canonical (deprecated dealer alias, legacy idempotency, legacy statuses, promotions not integrated)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.procure-to-pay.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (suppliers, POs, GRN, purchase invoices, auto-settle); 3 non-canonical (legacy X-Idempotency-Key rejected, PO/invoice no idempotency)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.invoice-dealer-finance.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (auto-issue during dispatch, portal finance ledger, dealer self-service, payments, PDF); 4 retired (dealer ledger/invoices/aging → portal finance, aging report → portal finance)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.accounting-period-close.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (journal CRUD, posting, period close, reconciliation, sub-ledger reconciliation); 3 non-canonical (period reopen unsupported, editing posted journals unsupported, automatic posting not implemented)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.hr-payroll.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (payroll run CRUD, calculation, posting, mark-paid, employees); 3 deprecated (legacy /hr/payroll-runs → /payroll/runs)
canonicalNonCanonicalSections.reporting-export.md: Section 6: 5 canonical paths (trial balance, balance sheet, GST return, export request, export download); 4 non-canonical (cash flow no date filter, aged-debtors vs aging/receivables split, account-statement dealer-only, audit digest deprecated)

crossModuleBoundaryNotes.auth-identity.md: Section 7: company (read), rbac (read), admin (write) — auth is platform boundary, no direct accounting dependency
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.tenant-admin-management.md: Section 7: auth (write), rbac (read), accounting (write for CoA/period), sales (read), portal (write)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.catalog-setup-readiness.md: Section 7: inventory (write mirror sync, read stock), factory (read), accounting (read)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.manufacturing-packing.md: Section 7: production/catalog (read), inventory (write consumption + FG batch), accounting (write WIP/cost journals)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.inventory-management.md: Section 7: factory (read batch source), sales (write reservations, read slips), purchasing (read batch source), accounting (write journals)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.order-to-cash.md: Section 7: inventory (write dispatch + COGS), accounting (write AR/revenue/settlements), invoice (write trigger), auth (write dealer user), factory (read slips)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.procure-to-pay.md: Section 7: inventory (write batch create + reduce), accounting (write AP journals + settlements)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.invoice-dealer-finance.md: Section 7: sales (trigger dispatch→invoice), accounting (write AR/settlement), inventory (read slip)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.accounting-period-close.md: Section 7: sales (trigger AR/revenue), purchasing (trigger AP), inventory (trigger adjustment), factory (trigger WIP), hr (trigger payroll)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.hr-payroll.md: Section 7: accounting (write post + payment reference), company (read gating + context)
crossModuleBoundaryNotes.reporting-export.md: Section 7: accounting (read journals), inventory (read stock), factory (read production), sales (read commercial), admin (read approval)

assertions.title: Each major flow doc records open decisions and incomplete seams

steps.action: Grep all flow packets for 'Open Decisions' section headers
steps.expected: Each flow packet has an open decisions section
steps.observed: All 11 flow packets contain an 'Open Decisions' section (Section 10 or 11 depending on packet structure)

steps.action: Grep all flow packets for 'Current Limitations' sections
steps.expected: Each flow packet records current limitations/incomplete seams
steps.observed: All 11 flow packets contain '### Current Limitations' under Section 5 (Completion Boundary / Current Definition of Done)

steps.action: Verify open decisions are tabular with status and notes columns
steps.expected: Open decisions table with Decision, Status, Notes
steps.observed: All open decisions sections use a table format with Decision, Status, and Notes columns

steps.action: Verify limitations describe incomplete seams, dead ends, and non-maintained behavior
steps.expected: Limitations identify specific incomplete or blocked behavior
steps.observed: Limitations are specific and numbered, identifying dead ends (e.g., company deletion blocked, zero-cost dispatch blocked), incomplete seams (e.g., no automated settlement, no event-driven confirmation), and non-maintained behavior (e.g., SecurityMonitoringService dead weight, MFA table unused)

currentLimitationsSummary.auth-identity.md: 5 limitations: MFA table unused (column used instead), refresh burns token before validation, lockout not enforced on existing tokens, profile changes not audited, brute-force monitoring inactive
currentLimitationsSummary.tenant-admin-management.md: 6 limitations: company deletion blocked, accounting cannot approve exports, main admin cannot self-deactivate, changelog no approval, support ticket sync best-effort, tenant limits not auto-enforced
currentLimitationsSummary.catalog-setup-readiness.md: 8 limitations: brand deactivation no cascade, item deactivation no cascade, readiness point-in-time, import no delete, search capped at 100, accounting metadata filtered, semi-finished SKU suffix reserved, inventory accounting events toggle
currentLimitationsSummary.manufacturing-packing.md: 6 limitations: production plan optional, no automated cost integration, wastage reason limited, idempotency key required (no fallback), batch number per month, packaging mapping mandatory
currentLimitationsSummary.inventory-management.md: 7 limitations: WAC cache 5-min TTL, zero-cost dispatch blocked, batch ID ambiguity, opening stock readiness gating, inventory accounting events toggle, no batch merge, no stock transfer
currentLimitationsSummary.order-to-cash.md: 7 limitations: no automated settlement, no automated order closure, no auto payment reconciliation, single domain event only, no shipment tracking, dunning rudimentary, no partial fulfillment by default
currentLimitationsSummary.procure-to-pay.md: 7 limitations: PO no idempotency, invoice no idempotency, GRN line-level only, no partial GRN per line, return stock reversal simple, no supplier dunning, supplier balance ledger-derived
currentLimitationsSummary.invoice-dealer-finance.md: 6 limitations: no auto payment reconciliation, settlement manual, no invoice approval workflow, limited invoice adjustment, aging hardcoded buckets, no partial payment allocation
currentLimitationsSummary.accounting-period-close.md: 7 limitations: no auto journal posting, period close manual, no scheduled reconciliation, correction via reversal only, opening balance limited, Tally import basic, no auto GST filing
currentLimitationsSummary.hr-payroll.md: 6 limitations: HR module paused by default, Indian regulatory only, no auto attendance-to-payroll sync, no employee self-service, no multi-currency, accounting-host payment seam
currentLimitationsSummary.reporting-export.md: 8 limitations: P&L no snapshot, cash flow no date filtering, cash flow heuristic, aging split ownership, balance sheet date drift, reconciliation dashboard session-tied, inventory recon no date filter, export approval configurable

openDecisionsSummary.auth-identity.md: 3 decisions: MFA recovery code table unused (known), profile audit gap (known), brute-force monitoring (unused)
openDecisionsSummary.tenant-admin-management.md: 3 decisions: company deletion (blocked), auto-limit enforcement (partial), changelog approval (none)
openDecisionsSummary.catalog-setup-readiness.md: 3 decisions: bulk variant dry-run (supported), deactivation cascade (not implemented), readiness caching (not implemented)
openDecisionsSummary.manufacturing-packing.md: 3 decisions: production plan usage (optional), cost integration (not automated), packing idempotency fallback (none)
openDecisionsSummary.inventory-management.md: 4 decisions: batch merge (not implemented), stock transfer (not implemented), zero-cost dispatch (blocked), intake toggle (configurable)
openDecisionsSummary.order-to-cash.md: 5 decisions: automated settlement (not implemented), automated order closure (not implemented), shipment tracking (not implemented), event-driven confirmation (not implemented), partial fulfillment (not default)
openDecisionsSummary.procure-to-pay.md: 4 decisions: PO idempotency (not implemented), invoice idempotency (not implemented), supplier dunning (not implemented), partial GRN per line (not supported)
openDecisionsSummary.invoice-dealer-finance.md: 4 decisions: auto payment reconciliation (not implemented), invoice approval (not implemented), credit notes (limited), custom aging buckets (not supported)
openDecisionsSummary.accounting-period-close.md: 4 decisions: auto journal posting (not implemented), scheduled reconciliation (not implemented), auto GST filing (not implemented), edit posted journals (not supported)
openDecisionsSummary.hr-payroll.md: 4 decisions: HR module default (paused by default), non-Indian payroll (not supported), employee self-service (not implemented), multi-currency (not supported)
openDecisionsSummary.reporting-export.md: 5 decisions: P&L snapshot (not implemented), cash flow date filtering (not implemented), cash flow tagging (not implemented), unified aging source (not implemented), historical reconciliation (not implemented)

assertions.title: Flow packets surface event/listener and access-control boundaries where they matter

steps.action: Grep all flow packets for 'Event/Listener Boundaries' section headers
steps.expected: Flow packets that materially depend on events/listeners have this section
steps.observed: 7 of 11 flow packets have explicit '## 8. Event/Listener Boundaries' sections: manufacturing-packing, inventory-management, order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, accounting-period-close, hr-payroll, reporting-export

steps.action: Verify event tables name specific event, listener, phase, and effect
steps.expected: Event tables with Event, Listener, Phase, Effect columns
steps.observed: All 7 packets with event sections have tables with Event | Listener | Phase | Effect columns naming specific Spring events (InventoryMovementEvent, InventoryValuationChangedEvent, SalesOrderCreatedEvent, JournalEntryPostedEvent) and listeners (InventoryAccountingEventListener, InventoryAuditListener, OrderAutoApprovalListener, SalesOrderCreatedAuditListener, JournalEntryPostedAuditListener)

steps.action: Verify event sections include key boundary notes about orchestration behavior
steps.expected: Key boundary notes explain hidden coupling and configuration guards
steps.observed: Each event section includes a 'Key boundary note' paragraph explaining: feature flag dependencies (erp.inventory.accounting.events.enabled), fail-open behavior, synchronous vs asynchronous patterns, REQUIRES_NEW transaction semantics

steps.action: Check packets without explicit event sections for access-control boundaries
steps.expected: Packets without material event dependencies still document access-control gates
steps.observed: 4 packets (auth-identity, tenant-admin-management, catalog-setup-readiness, invoice-dealer-finance) have '## 8. Security Considerations' with RBAC/access-control notes. Auth-identity has RBAC per entrypoint. Invoice-dealer-finance documents host isolation (/api/v1/portal/finance/** vs /api/v1/dealer-portal/**). Catalog-setup documents class-level RBAC and write restrictions.

steps.action: Verify access-control boundaries are documented where non-obvious gates exist
steps.expected: Non-obvious access-control gates are explicitly named
steps.observed: OPERATIONAL_DISPATCH role predicate for dispatch (inventory, O2C); auto-approval listener conditional on SystemSettingsService.isAutoApprovalEnabled() (O2C); export approval gate with exportApprovalRequired toggle (reporting); HR module gating per tenant (HR/payroll); dealer self-service auto-scoping via JWT (invoice/O2C)

eventListenerSections.manufacturing-packing.md: Section 8: InventoryMovementEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), InventoryValuationChangedEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT). Key boundary note on erp.inventory.accounting.events.enabled toggle.
eventListenerSections.inventory-management.md: Section 8: InventoryMovementEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), InventoryValuationChangedEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), InventoryMovementEvent → InventoryAuditListener (AFTER_COMMIT). Plus access-control boundaries: OPERATIONAL_DISPATCH predicate, RBAC by operation, idempotency required.
eventListenerSections.order-to-cash.md: Section 8: SalesOrderCreatedEvent → OrderAutoApprovalListener (AFTER_COMMIT), SalesOrderCreatedEvent → SalesOrderCreatedAuditListener (AFTER_COMMIT). Key boundary note: only SalesOrderCreatedEvent published; confirmation/dispatch/cancellation operate via direct service calls, not events.
eventListenerSections.procure-to-pay.md: Section 8: InventoryMovementEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), InventoryValuationChangedEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT). Key boundary note on GRN stock truth boundary and feature flag dependency.
eventListenerSections.accounting-period-close.md: Section 8: InventoryMovementEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), InventoryValuationChangedEvent → InventoryAccountingEventListener (AFTER_COMMIT), JournalEntryPostedEvent → JournalEntryPostedAuditListener (AFTER_COMMIT, fallbackExecution=true). Key boundary notes: REQUIRES_NEW transaction semantics, configuration-guarded fail-open behavior.
eventListenerSections.hr-payroll.md: Section 8: SalesOrderCreatedEvent → OrderAutoApprovalListener (AFTER_COMMIT) — indirect connection only. Key boundary note: payroll does not publish events; is primarily a consumer of accounting services.
eventListenerSections.reporting-export.md: Section 8: JournalEntryPostedEvent → Report queries (read-time). Access-control boundaries: export approval gate, RBAC, statement PDFs bypass approval, split aging ownership. Key boundary note on live vs snapshot report data.

accessControlOnlyPackets.auth-identity.md: Section 8 Security Considerations: bearer token auth, HS256 JWT, company-context filter, rate limiting, token blacklist. Roles per actor in Section 1.
accessControlOnlyPackets.tenant-admin-management.md: Section 8 Security Considerations: super-admin auth, tenant isolation, session revocation, export approval gate, company context filter. Roles per actor in Section 1.
accessControlOnlyPackets.catalog-setup-readiness.md: Section 8 Security Considerations: class-level RBAC, write restriction to ADMIN/ACCOUNTING, accounting metadata visibility filtering.
accessControlOnlyPackets.invoice-dealer-finance.md: Section 8 Security Considerations: host isolation (/api/v1/portal/finance/** vs /api/v1/dealer-portal/**), RBAC, dealer auto-scoping via JWT, cross-dealer prevention, PDF audit logging.

summary: Tested 3 assertions across 11 major flow packets. All 3 assertions PASS. VAL-FLOW-004: All 11 flow packets have Section 6 'Canonical vs Non-Canonical Paths' with explicit canonical path tables (owning controller/module), non-canonical/deprecated path tables (with replacement notes or no-replacement status), and Section 7 'Cross-Module Dependencies' identifying entrypoint ownership and downstream side-effect direction. VAL-FLOW-005: All 11 flow packets have 'Current Limitations' subsections and 'Open Decisions' sections recording incomplete seams, dead ends, blocked behavior, and non-maintained surfaces with explicit status (not implemented, blocked, partial, configurable). VAL-FLOW-007: 7 of 11 packets have dedicated 'Event/Listener Boundaries' sections (Section 8) naming specific Spring events and listeners with phase, effect, and key boundary notes; the remaining 4 packets (auth, tenant-admin, catalog, invoice-finance) do not materially depend on internal events and instead document access-control gates in Security Considerations sections. 9 named access-control gates were identified across all packets.
